narrative_blocks.header: He was Arizona's first veterinary surgeon
narrative_blocks.body: A McGill-trained veterinarian, Chandler came to Arizona Territory in 1887 as its first territorial veterinary surgeon. Within months he resigned to chase the opportunities of a fast-growing frontier.

narrative_blocks.header: He turned desert into farmland with canals
narrative_blocks.body: Chandler studied the new science of irrigation engineering and built one of the Southwest's largest canal systems through his Consolidated Canal Company. By 1900 he owned an eighteen-thousand-acre ranch in the Salt River Valley.

narrative_blocks.header: He founded the city of Chandler, Arizona
narrative_blocks.body: In 1912 Chandler subdivided his ranch and opened a townsite that drew three hundred speculators on its first day. He planned an elegant town square and befriended Frank Lloyd Wright, with whom he sketched a dozen unbuilt projects.

narrative_blocks.header: He once staged an ostrich drive
narrative_blocks.body: Among his stranger ventures, Chandler bought an entire ostrich company and tried to drive the birds like cattle some twenty-seven miles across the desert. He was also among the first to grow Egyptian long-staple cotton in Arizona.

narrative_blocks.header: She married the founder of Chandler, Arizona
narrative_blocks.body: Julia first married Dr. Alexander Chandler, the developer who founded the Arizona city that bears his name. She later married William Loftus and settled in California.
